title: Paphiopedilum wolterianum
taxonomy: Paphiopedilum wolterianum is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, specifically within the genus Paphiopedilum. This genus is further divided into several subgenera, with Paphiopedilum wolterianum belonging to the subgenus Brachypetalum. Within the subgenus Brachypetalum, Paphiopedilum wolterianum is grouped as one of the many species characterized by its unique flower morphology and growth habits. The classification of Paphiopedilum wolterianum is based on its distinctive features and its evolutionary relationships with other species in the genus. As a result, Paphiopedilum wolterianum is an integral part of the orchid family tree, with its classification serving as a framework for understanding its biology and conservation status.

intro: Paphiopedilum wolterianum, also known as Wolter's Venus slipper, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Paphiopedilum wolterianum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, this plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions.
